Interim Compliance Date Re: Paga Settlement (Set Per May-30-2019 Hearing)
Off Calendar Feb-20-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Michael D. Early, Esq. of KLEIN HOCKEL IEZZA & PATEL, T:415.951.0535, for plaintiff;
No other appearance.

INTERIM COMPLIANCE HEARING RE: PAGA SETTLEMENT (Set Per May-30-2019 Hearing)

Hearing was held. Plaintiff's counsel updated the Court as to the number of checks that were undeliverable, the number of checks that were not cashed and the check cashing procedure. The Court was also apprised that information was provided to the class members should they require assistance in any way and that counsel would be available for that purpose.

Therefore, to the satisfaction of the Court, the PAGA Settlement was declared compliant and completed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar May-31-2019 Continued To Aug-06-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Plaintiff Matthew Del Valle Motion - Notice Of Motion To Approve Paga Settlement
Off Calendar May-30-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Michael Early, Esq. 415-951-0535 of Klein Hockel Iezza Patel for Plaintiff.
Christopher Ward, Esq. 312-832-4364 of Foley & Lardner LLP for Defendant through Court Call.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff Matthew Del Valle's Motion To Approve Paga Settlement.

Plaintiff Matthew DelValle's motion for approval of the PAGA settlement agreement, as amended, is granted. The Court finds that the overall amount of the proposed settlement is fair and adequate in view of the purposes and policies of PAGA, taking into account such factors as the adequacy of the civil penalties in light of the potential maximum if the matter were to go to verdict and the risks of litigation. (Lab. Code 2699(l)(2); see Flores v. Starwood Hotels & Resorts Worldwide, Inc. (C.D. Cal. 2017) 253 F.Supp.3d 1074, 1077; O'Connor v. Uber Technologies, Inc. (N.D. Cal. 2016) 201 F.Supp.3d 1110, 1132-35.)

Plaintiff's counsel is awarded $1,612,500 in attorneys' fees and $18,718 in costs, which the Court finds to be a reasonable amount. (Lab. Code 2699(g)(1).) The award represents 30% of the Gross Settlement Amount of $5,375,000, the common fund created by the settlement, an amount that the Court finds to be appropriate under the circumstances of this case. (See Laffitte v. Robert Half Int'l Inc. (2016) 1 Cal.5th 480.) The Court has cross-checked the reasonableness of this fee utilizing the lodestar-multiplier method. Based on the materials submitted with the moving and supplemental papers, the Court finds that Mr. Early's reasonable hourly rate is at least $750 per hour, and that the amount of time spent by counsel on the case was exceedingly reasonable, yielding a total lodestar of at least $358,065. The percentage fee award thus equates to a multiplier of 4.5 or less. Such a multiplier, while high, is appropriate in the circumstances of this case given the contingent risk assumed by counsel, the quality of the representation, and the results obtained, particularly in comparison to those achieved in two class actions raising closely similar claims against one of the same defendants. (Early Decl. 31-35.) Consistent with the settlement agreement, the balance of the $2,150,000 originally requested by counsel as attorneys' fees and costs shall be included in the Net Settlement Amount to be paid to the State of California and the PAGA Class Members.

In addition, an Interim Compliance Date is set for November 12, 2109 at 9:30 am in Dept. 302.

Stipulated order signed. (see scanned order for details)Plaintiff to submit a proposed judgment in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

Plaintiff Matthew Del Valle Motion - Notice Of Motion To Approve Paga Settlement
Off Calendar May-16-2019 Continued To May-30-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Michael D. Early, Esq., Klein Hockel, et al, Telephone 415-951-0535 appearing for Plaintiff;
Christopher G. Ward, Esq., Foley & Lardner, LLP, Telephone 312-832-4364, appearing for Defendant Menzies Aviation (CourtCall);

Matter is called at 1:30 p.m. and a hearing is held on Plaintiff Matthew Del Valle's Motion To Approve Paga Settlement.

The Court adresses the issues raised in its tenative ruling with counsel present at today's hearing. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)
